fields.name: defaultBranch
fields.doc: from the forge (`repos/{o}/{r}`), when a GitHub sync has run

fields.name: homepageUrl
fields.doc: the repo's homepage URL from the forge, when set

entities.name: GitNote
entities.doc:
A git note (`refs/notes/*`): free-form text attached to any object after the
fact, without rewriting it. Mutable per-repo state like refs — each sync
replaces the repo's rows. The default ref is `refs/notes/commits`; other
namespaces ride the notesRef column.

fields.name: notesRef
fields.doc: full notes ref name, e.g. `refs/notes/commits`

fields.name: annotatedOid
fields.doc: the object the note annotates (usually a commit)

entities.name: GitObject
entities.doc:
The git object family — the polymorphic root (DESIGN §2 Layer B). A ref or a
tree entry may point at ANY object; the family guarantees one key type (Oid)
so a polymorphic reference is the typeable pair (type, oid). Concrete leaves:
Commit, Tree, Blob. (Annotated tag objects are a known future member —
today's ingest resolves refs straight to commits; see FINDINGS #7.)

fields.name: oid
fields.doc: object id — the content hash (binary; hex via `lower(hex(oid))`). Globally unique, so the family key is oid alone.

fields.name: repo
fields.doc: the repo this object was seen in (an association, not a key member)

entities.name: Commit
entities.doc:
One row per commit, walked from every ref. Author and committer time/identity
are kept separate; `summary` is the first line of `message`.

fields.name: tree
fields.doc: root tree this commit points at

fields.name: message
fields.doc: full commit message

fields.name: summary
fields.doc: first line of the message

fields.name: isMerge
fields.doc:
The §9.3 v1 carve-out: a REAL derived field — true iff a second parent
exists. Today it is ALSO a stored column (the source computes it; parity
with the hand-written templates) and the codec additionally emits a
`commits_derived` view computing it from `parents`. At the Step-5 swap the
stored column drops and the view becomes the only projection.

fields.name: parents
fields.doc: this commit's parents, ordered by `idx` (merges have several)

fields.name: entries
fields.doc:
The polymorphic edge already in production: tree_entries stores the
(type, key) pair as (entry_type, child_oid) — exactly DESIGN §2's
projection of a polymorphic association.

fields.name: content
fields.doc: raw file bytes (object ingest / --objects); enables `entl rebuild`

entities.name: FileChange
entities.doc:
Per-commit file changes — a WEAK ENTITY: identified by (owner, path), no
global id of its own. Authored as an entity with FK-in-PK; whether it should
*also* be a

entities.name: Ref
entities.doc: Branches, tags, remote-tracking refs, and HEAD — one row each.

fields.name: name
fields.doc: short ref name, e.g. `main` or `origin/main`

fields.name: target
fields.doc:
Today: implicitly commit-typed (no discriminator column). The honest model
is `target: GitObject` — an annotated-tag ref targets a tag object, not a
commit — but that adds a target_type column, i.e. a real schema change.
Authored as Commit for byte parity; the upgrade is FINDINGS #7.

entities.name: Conflict
entities.doc:
Merge-conflict hot zones (the north star). Populated by the `entl conflicts`
pass: replay every historical 2-parent merge with gix's 3-way tree merge and
record the paths that conflicted. `unresolved` = git would have required a
human, vs auto-resolved.

fields.name: mergeOid
fields.doc: hex text today, while every other oid column is blob — schema inconsistency, FINDINGS #8

entities.name: GhSubject
entities.doc:
The PR/Issue family — the SECOND polymorphic root already in production:
gh_comments / gh_labeled / gh_assignees reference a subject as the pair
(subject_type, subject_number) against this family's shared key
(repo, number). Root carries ONLY the key: sharing data fields via
inheritance would break per-leaf column-order parity (FINDINGS #6).

entities.name: GhPullRequest
entities.doc:
Pull requests and their lifecycle. `mergeable` + `checks` are the live
conflict and CI signals; head/base oids drive on-demand base...head diffs.

fields.name: checks
fields.doc: head commit CI rollup: SUCCESS | FAILURE | PENDING | … (open set — stays text)

fields.name: head
fields.doc: PR head commit (for base...head diffs)

fields.name: base
fields.doc: base branch tip at fetch time

fields.name: commits
fields.doc: plain join table (PK = all columns): the PR's commits

fields.name: requestedReviewers
fields.doc: plain join table: requested reviewers

entities.name: GhComment
entities.doc: Issue/PR comments — subject is a polymorphic reference into the GhSubject family.

fields.name: subject
fields.doc: the (type, key) pair: (subject_type, repo_id + subject_number)

entities.name: GhPrReview
entities.doc: PR reviews — globally id-keyed, referencing the PR by its composite key.

fields.name: pr
fields.doc: multi-column FK to a composite-keyed entity (repo_id, pr_number)

fields.name: inReplyTo
fields.doc: threaded replies — a nullable self-association

entities.name: GhEvent
entities.doc:
Raw GitHub event stream (the /events feed) — entl's "did anything happen?"
signal AND a queryable activity log. The feed is capped (~300 events / 90
days): complete going FORWARD from when polling starts.

fields.name: actor
fields.doc: denormalized pair: the association + a plain login column (FINDINGS #9)

fields.name: payload
fields.doc: the event's type-specific JSON

entities.name: GhStep
entities.doc: Weak entity again: a step is (job, number), no global id.

valueStructs.name: SinkStats
valueStructs.doc: What a sink() cycle produced: git + forge counts, and rows applied to the target.

valueStructs.name: FileDiff
valueStructs.doc: One changed file between two commits.

fields.name: patch
fields.doc: full single-file unified patch; empty for binary files (matching GitHub)

valueStructs.name: ChangeBatch
valueStructs.doc:
One change-stream batch. The rows stay columnar (Arrow): `ipc` yields them as
one Arrow IPC stream (a single RecordBatch, schema included), and the Python
class additionally speaks the Arrow PyCapsule protocol for zero-copy import
(`pa.record_batch(batch)`). Values are native Arrow types — oids are Binary,
timestamps Timestamp(µs) — unlike query()/extract()'s canonical JSON (hex
oids, RFC3339). No per-row `_op`: `op`/`table` ride this envelope.

valueStructs.name: Statement
valueStructs.doc: One driver-plan statement: the host runs it verbatim against its own client.

fields.name: table
fields.doc: canonical source table (null for cross-table DDL) — for per-table tallies

fields.name: path
fields.doc: the SQLite file, the JSONL directory, or the Postgres URL

fields.name: github
fields.doc: also pull GitHub (default true; needs a token)

fields.name: tables
fields.doc: only write these tables (default: all)

fields.name: schema
fields.doc: target schema (Postgres only; default "entl")

fields.name: objects
fields.doc: also store the object graph (trees/blobs + raw content) so the store can rebuild the repo

fields.name: source
fields.doc: duckdb | sqlite | jsonl | postgres

fields.name: source
fields.doc:
duckdb | sqlite | jsonl | postgres. (Named `source`, not `from`: `from` is a
Python keyword and awkward in JS — the op surface stays binding-friendly.)

fields.name: out
fields.doc: output directory for the reconstructed repo
